data.description:
## Why

The GitHub mirror is the public discovery point, yet it lacks the standard
community-health files contributors and security researchers expect. There is no
`SECURITY.md`, `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, issue templates, PR template, or
`SUPPORT.md`. Their absence reads as "not ready for outside contribution" and
leaves vulnerability reporters with no documented channel.

## What

Add GitHub-native community health files that meet contributors where they are
while preserving the Gerrit merge gate:

- `SECURITY.md` — primary reporting via **GitHub private vulnerability reporting**
  (Security tab / advisories); fallback contact `hello@navapbc.com`; a supported-
  versions note and a coordinated-disclosure statement.
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` — **Contributor Covenant 2.1** verbatim, enforcement
  contact `hello@navapbc.com`.
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml` + `feature_request.yml` — GitHub
  **issue forms**, plus `config.yml` with `blank_issues_enabled: false` and
  contact links to `CONTRIBUTING.md` (the Gerrit flow) and `docs/`.
- `.github/pull_request_template.md` — a **redirect** template stating GitHub PRs
  are not merged here and pointing to the Gerrit review flow in `CONTRIBUTING.md`
  (NOT an auto-mirror bot).
- `SUPPORT.md` — where to get help (issues for bugs/features; docs links; code
  contributions go through Gerrit).

## Scope

Docs/meta files only. No code changes. The redirect PR template is the
deliberate substitute for the out-of-scope PR->Gerrit mirror bot. `hello@navapbc.com`
is used per maintainer decision; add a code comment noting it should be confirmed
to route before the first public release.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SECURITY.md` exists with GitHub private vuln reporting as the primary
  channel, `hello@navapbc.com` fallback, supported-versions + disclosure policy.
- [ ] `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` is Contributor Covenant 2.1 with the enforcement
  contact filled in.
- [ ] `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` has bug + feature issue forms and a `config.yml`
  that disables blank issues and links to CONTRIBUTING/docs.
- [ ] `.github/pull_request_template.md` redirects contributors to the Gerrit flow.
- [ ] `SUPPORT.md` exists and links docs + CONTRIBUTING.
- [ ] GitHub's community-standards checklist shows these files detected.
data.title: Add GitHub community health files (SECURITY, CODE_OF_CONDUCT, issue/PR templates, SUPPORT)
